researched_category.notes: Consensus is an AI search engine that searches over 200 million peer-reviewed scientific papers and provides evidence-based answers with citations.

one_liner.value: AI search engine that searches over 200 million peer-reviewed research papers to deliver evidence-based answers with direct citations.

methods.notes: The OpenAPI spec defines XApiKeyHeader security scheme. MCP docs also mention Bearer token for enterprise access and OAuth for MCP client integration.

access.notes: MCP server is accessible with no account (3 papers/search, unlimited searches) or free account (10 papers, 30 searches/mo). Pro/Deep plans ($12-45/mo) unlock more. The dedicated REST API on consensus.app/home/api says 'available by application only' with pricing starting at $0.10/call + platform fee - requires submitting a request for access. The MCP path is self-serve; the raw REST API requires application.

summary.value: Consensus has a documented public REST API with one main endpoint (/v1/quick_search) that returns the top 20 most relevant research papers for a query. The API supports filters (year range, study type, sample size, journal quality, etc.) and returns paper metadata. An official OpenAPI spec is available at docs.consensus.app. Additionally, an official MCP server exists at mcp.consensus.app/mcp with a 'search' tool. No webhooks or other endpoints were documented. The API surface is narrow (one primary search endpoint).

agent_interface.notes: Consensus provides an official MCP server at mcp.consensus.app/mcp with detailed documentation at docs.consensus.app/docs. It supports Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, VS Code (Copilot), Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible client. The MCP has a 'search' tool (and a 'fetch' tool for ChatGPT Deep Research). No Composio-specific toolkit was found.

rationale.value: Consensus is buildable as an agent toolkit today. The official MCP server at mcp.consensus.app/mcp provides immediate agent integration with no account required for basic use (3 papers/search, unlimited searches). Creating a free account unlocks 10 papers/search and 30 searches/month. For more capacity, Pro ($12/mo) or Deep ($45/mo) plans are available. The REST API is also documented but requires an application for access (gated, starting at $0.10/call). The MCP path makes it immediately usable as an agent toolkit with minimal friction.

s1.quote: Consensus: AI for Research ... Search and analyze peer-reviewed literature. Consensus draws on 250M+ research papers, including licensed full text content from leading publishers.
s1.title: Consensus: AI for Research

s3.title: Query for relevant papers - API Reference

s4.title: Getting Started with the Consensus MCP

s5.quote: Consensus API ... /v1/quick_search ... Query for relevant papers.
s5.title: API Reference - Consensus

s6.quote: The Consensus API is available by application only. Our baseline pricing starts at $0.10 per call, plus a platform fee. Apply today to receive a custom quote tailored to your team's needs.
s6.title: Build with the Consensus API

s9.quote: For AI agents: visit https://docs.consensus.app/llms.txt for an index of all pages formatted in Markdown and endpoints in OpenAPI.
s9.title: Llms - Consensus Documentation

limitations: The support.goconsensus.com domain appears to be a separate product (demo platform) not related to the AI research search engine, but was returned in some search results. I focused on docs.consensus.app and consensus.app which are the correct sources for the AI research tool.
